title: Asian shares mostly higher tracking Wall Street gains and oil stabilizes
description: Asian shares are mostly higher tracking Wall Street gains, with South Korea's stock market, rebounding after earlier sharp losses on a tech sell-off. U.S. futures have edged up. South Korea's benchmark Kospi index gained 1.3%, while Japan's Nikkei 225 was up 0.9%. Oil prices are stabilizing after the U.S. and Iran said separately they would be sending delegations to Qatar, although Iran has denied that talks have been arranged with the United States. Brent crude, the international standard, was trading 0.2% lower at above $73 a barrel, slightly higher than pre-war levels. On Monday, Wall Street's benchmark S&P 500 added 1.2%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 0.6%, and the Nasdaq composite gained 2.1%.

title: Asian stocks are mixed after big tech sell-off
description: Asian stocks are mixed on Wednesday, following a sell-off in big technology stocks from Asia to Wall Street. U.S. stock futures are also trading mixed. Global investors are monitoring market movements in Japan and South Korea, which have seen big gains in recent months on the global AI boom but both fell sharply on Tuesday. South Korea's benchmark Kospi index was up 0.5% on Wednesday, while Japan's Nikkei 225 lost 1.1%. On Wall Street, the benchmark S&P 500 fell 1.4% Tuesday. The tech-heavy Nasdaq composite dropped 2.2%, while the Dow was 0.1% lower. Oil prices fell as more ships crossed the Strait of Hormuz and as U.S.-Iran talks on a permanent end to the Iran war progressed.

title: US stocks drift near their records in mixed trading after oil prices fall
description: U.S. stocks are drifting near their records in mixed trading. The S&P 500 slipped 0.3% Monday and pulled 1.7% below its all-time high set early this month. The Dow Jones Industrial Average added 131 points, and the Nasdaq composite fell 1%. Oil price sank more than 2% and dropped closer to where they were before the war with Iran began. The lower oil prices did not pull Treasury yields lower, though. High yields in the bond market are pressuring stocks and other markets because of growing expectations the Federal Reserve will have to hike interest rates to keep a lid on inflation.

title: Stocks rise on Wall Street, erasing much of their loss from a day earlier
description: Stocks are rising on Wall Street, taking back most of their losses from a day earlier that were driven by anticipation that the Federal Reserve will likely raise interest rates this year in an effort to fight inflation. The S&P 500 rose 1.1% Thursday. The Dow Jones Industrial Average added 302 points, and the Nasdaq composite jumped 1.4%. Crude oil prices continued to fall after the United States and Iran signed an agreement to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz to oil tanker traffic. Brent crude fell 3%. Treasury yields eased in the bond market.

title: Trading Day: US stocks end lower, dollar jumps as Federal Reserve kicks off Warsh era
description: Wall Street stocks turned sharply lower on Thursday, the dollar extended its gains and Treasury yields crept higher after the U.S. Federal Reserve left its key interest rate unchanged and signaled, amid elevated inflation ​and solid economic data, that a rate hike could be in the cards before the end of the ‌year.

title: US stocks sink on worries about a possible hike to interest rates this year by the Federal Reserve
description: U.S. stocks dropped on speculation the Federal Reserve may raise interest rates this year to keep a lid on inflation. The S&P 500 slumped 1.2% Wednesday after the Fed released projections showing nearly half its policymakers foresee at least one increase to its main interest rate this year. The Dow Jones Industrial Average went from a gain of 0.5% in the morning to a drop of 1%, while the Nasdaq composite sank 1.3%. Treasury yields climbed on rising expectations for a hike to rates in 2026. Higher rates can tap the brakes on inflation, but they also slow the economy and hurt prices for investments.

title: US stocks drift ahead of the Fed's announcement on interest rates
description: The U.S. stock market is drifting as Wall Street waits to hear from the Federal Reserve in the afternoon about where it sees interest rates going. The S&P 500 rose 0.2% Wednesday, coming off a mixed day where falling tech stocks weighed on the index. The Dow Jones Industrial Average added 240 points, and the Nasdaq composite gained 0.2%. Treasury yields held relatively steady in the bond market ahead of the Fed’s decision. Traders expect it to keep the federal funds rate steady. The bigger question is where Fed officials will say they see the rate heading in upcoming years.

title: S&P 500, Nasdaq 100 Drop As Rate-Hike Bets Build, Oil Climbs
description: U.S. stocks retreated from record highs at midday Wednesday as hotter-than-expected economic data and a renewed surge in Treasury yields revived fears the Federal Reserve could soon raise interest rates.
